full_suite.command: jq '.status, (.mechanism_table | length), (.clips | keys)' runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/playback_decision_table.json && ls -l runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708 && wc -l runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/playback_decision_table.md runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/playback_decision_table.json && rg -n "Recommendation|recommendation" runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/playback_decision_table.md runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/playback_decision_table.json
full_suite.failure_notes: Read-only lane, so no project test suite was run. JSON parsed and reported 4 mechanisms/4 clips; artifact files exist. The rg command intentionally returned no matches for recommendation text, confirming the deliverable has no recommendation section.

honest_issues: The exact close-proof dense monolith sizes could not be measured because those runs explicitly did not build/fetch body_mesh.json or smpl_motion.json in speed-default mode. I reported older local monoliths only where present and labeled them as non-close-proof.
Mesh cap extrapolation is linear from measured body_mesh_index variable bytes plus one shared faces file. It is a storage/transfer estimate, not a fresh exported artifact measurement for raised caps.
GPU cost estimates use measured steady H100 ms/person-frame. Full BODY wall time includes setup/preprocessing/gates/index/transfer and should not be scaled from that steady component alone.
The repo had unrelated dirty changes outside this lane in git status; I did not modify them.
next: Manager can use the table to rule P2-2 wiring/menu ordering. Proposed BUILD_CHECKLIST bullet is included verbatim in both artifacts.
